121:   - Summary:
122:     The Coaching Plugin is an OpenCode plugin that provides real-time behavioral feedback to orchestrators and workers through tool usage pattern detection. It implements the "Pain as Signal" architectural pattern: agents should feel friction in real-time rather than learning about it post-hoc.
123:     
124:     The plugin hooks `tool.execute.after` to observe tool usage patterns (it cannot see LLM response text—fundamental constraint), detects 8 behavioral patterns using behavioral proxies (action ratio, analysis paralysis, frame collapse, etc.), and injects coaching messages via `client.session.prompt({ noReply: true })`. Metrics persist to `~/.orch/coaching-metrics.jsonl` and are exposed via `/api/coaching` for dashboard visualization.
125:     
126:     **Current status (Feb 2026):** Both orchestrator and worker coaching operational. Orchestrator coaching: 1000+ metrics collected. Worker health tracking: verified working Feb 14 — stress test (50+ tool calls) emitted `context_usage` worker metric with zero orchestrator metric leakage. Fix required two opencode fork commits (459a1bfba, 0922edfe7) to: (1) read `x-opencode-env-ORCH_WORKER` header and set `session.metadata.role='worker'`, (2) pass `session.metadata` through `tool.execute.after` plugin hooks. **Note:** Worker detection only works for headless (OpenCode HTTP API) spawns — Claude CLI/tmux spawns bypass the HTTP session creation endpoint and don't get metadata set.
127:     
128:     ---
129:   - Critical Invariants:
130:     1. **Plugins cannot see LLM response text** - Only tool calls visible, not free-text responses. Fundamental constraint, not fixable.
131:     2. **Behavioral proxies are the only detection mechanism** - All pattern detection uses tool usage as proxy signals.
132:     3. **Metrics persist, session state doesn't** - JSONL file survives restarts, in-memory Map is ephemeral.
133:     4. **Observation coupled to intervention** - Injection only fires from `flushMetrics` within `tool.execute.after` hook.
134:     5. **Worker detection caching is one-way** - Only cache `true` results (confirmed worker), never cache `false`.
135:     6. **Two injection mechanisms serve different purposes** - `config.instructions` adds file references at config time (static context like skills), `client.session.prompt(noReply: true)` injects content at runtime (immediate coaching).
136:     
137:     ---
138:   - Why This Fails:
139:     ### Failure Mode 1: Worker Detection Caching Bug (Jan 10-17)
140:     
141:     **Symptom:** Zero worker health metrics in production despite implemented code
142:     
143:     **Root cause:** `detectWorkerSession()` cached both `true` AND `false` results, permanently misclassifying workers if ANY tool call happened before a worker-identifying signal
144:     
145:     **Why it happens:**
146:     1. Worker session starts, first tool = `glob` (no detection signal)
147:     2. `isWorker = false` → cached → function returns `false` forever
148:     3. Second tool = `read SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` → cached result returned, detection skipped
149:     4. Worker treated as orchestrator for entire session
150:     
151:     **Cascade:**
152:     ```
153:     First non-matching tool call → cache false → subsequent detection signals ignored → worker health code never runs
154:     ```
155:     
156:     **Fix (Jan 17):** Only cache `true` results (confirmed worker), never cache `false`. Allow re-evaluation on each tool call until worker confirmed.
157:     
158:     **Pattern established:** "Never cache negative results in per-session detection"
159:     
160:     ---
161:     
162:     ### Failure Mode 2: Invalid Detection Signal (Bash workdir)
163:     
164:     **Symptom:** Detection code exists but never fires
165:     
166:     **Root cause:** Detection checked for `args.workdir` on bash tool, but bash tool has no `workdir` argument
167:     
168:     **Why it happens:**
169:     - Bash tool args are: `command`, `timeout`, `dangerouslyDisableSandbox`, `run_in_background`
170:     - No `workdir` argument exists
171:     - Detection signal `if (tool === "bash" && args?.workdir)` never matches
172:     
173:     **Fix (Jan 17):** Removed broken detection signal, restored file-path detection for any `.orch/workspace/` path
174:     
175:     ---
176:     
177:     ### Failure Mode 3: Observation Coupled to Intervention (Restart Brittleness)
178:     
179:     **Symptom:** Coaching messages stop after OpenCode server restart, even though metrics show problems
180:     
181:     **Root cause:** Injection is implemented as side effect of metric collection, not as separate concern that can operate independently
182:     
183:     **Why it happens:**
184:     - Metrics are **persistent** (JSONL file, survive restart)
185:     - Session state is **in-memory** (Map, lost on restart)
186:     - Injection logic **coupled to metric collection** (only happens via `flushMetrics`)
187:     - After restart: metrics file shows "poor" status, but no session state exists, so injection never fires
188:     
189:     **Cascade:**
190:     ```
191:     Server restart → session state lost → flushMetrics not called → injection doesn't fire → coaching stops
192:     ```
193:     
194:     **Architectural fix (not yet implemented):** Separate injection into independent daemon that reads metrics from JSONL and injects via OpenCode API, completely decoupled

204: - System Learning Loop
205:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/system-learning-loop.md
206:   - Summary:
207:     The System Learning Loop is the third layer of the Pressure Visibility System that automatically converts recurring context gaps into actionable improvements. It tracks gaps during spawns, identifies patterns using RecurrenceThreshold=3, and suggests specific actions (kn entries, beads issues, investigations). The system uses shell-aware command parsing to generate runnable commands with proper quoted string handling, and ensures minimum length requirements for downstream tools (kn requires 20+ chars). This creates a closed feedback loop: gaps → patterns → suggestions → improvements → fewer gaps.
208:     
209:     ---
210:   - Critical Invariants:
211:     1. **RecurrenceThreshold = 3** - Pattern detection balances noise (1) vs signal (3+)
212:     2. **All matching events must be marked resolved** - Not just the most recent one
213:     3. **FindRecurringGaps excludes resolved events** - Prevents resolved gaps from reappearing
214:     4. **Shell-aware command parsing required** - Quoted strings with spaces must be preserved
215:     5. **Minimum 20-character reasons** - kn decide/constrain requirement enforced at generation time
216:     6. **30-day retention window** - Gap events older than 30 days are pruned
217:     7. **Gap recording happens after gating** - Captures all gaps whether spawn proceeds or not
218:     
219:     ---
220:   - Why This Fails:
221:     ### Failure Mode 1: Resolved Gaps Keep Appearing
222:     
223:     **Symptom:** `orch learn` shows same gap after running `orch learn act` to resolve it
224:     
225:     **Root cause:** Two bugs combined:
226:     1. `RecordResolution` only marked the most recent event (used `break` after first match)
227:     2. `FindRecurringGaps` counted all events without filtering by Resolution field
228:     
229:     **Why it happens:**
230:     - Gap occurs 5 times → user resolves → only 1 event marked
231:     - Next `FindRecurringGaps` call counts 4 unresolved events → still above threshold (3)
232:     - Same pattern keeps appearing despite resolution
233:     
234:     **Impact:**
235:     - User frustration ("I already fixed this!")
236:     - Loss of trust in learning system
237:     - Duplicate work
238:     
239:     **Fix:**
240:     - `RecordResolution` now marks ALL matching events (removed `break`)
241:     - `FindRecurringGaps` filters out resolved events before counting
242:     
243:     **Source:** `.kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-orch-learn-resolved-gaps-still.md`
244:     
245:     ---
246:     
247:     ### Failure Mode 2: Generated Commands Fail Due to Broken Quoting
248:     
249:     **Symptom:** `orch learn act N` generates command that fails when executed
250:     
251:     **Root cause:** Using `strings.Fields()` to parse commands - splits on whitespace without respecting quotes
252:     
253:     **Why it happens:**
254:     - Command: `kn decide "auth" --reason "Used by: investigation. Occurred 5 times"`
255:     - `strings.Fields` splits into: `["kn", "decide", "\"auth\"", "--reason", "\"Used", "by:", "investigation.", ...]`
256:     - Shell receives mangled arguments, command fails
257:     
258:     **Impact:**
259:     - Learning loop broken - suggestions can't be executed
260:     - User must manually reconstruct commands
261:     - Reduces value of automated suggestions
262:     
263:     **Fix:**
264:     - Added `ParseShellCommand()` with shell-aware quote handling
265:     - Respects double and single quotes as argument delimiters
266:     - Added `ValidateCommand()` to catch malformed commands before execution
267:     
268:     **Source:** `.kb/investigations/2025-12-26-inv-orch-learn-act-commands-should.md`
269:     
270:     ---
271:     
272:     ### Failure Mode 3: Short Reasons Fail kn Validation
273:     
274:     **Symptom:** `orch learn act` generates kn command that fails with "reason too short" error
275:     
276:     **Root cause:** `generateReasonFromGaps` produced "Occurred N times" (16 chars) when gap events lacked skill/task metadata
277:     
278:     **Why it happens:**
279:     - Gap events with sparse metadata → only occurrence count available
280:     - "Occurred 3 times" = 16 characters
281:     - kn requires 20+ characters for `--reason` flag
282:     - Command fails validation at execution time
283:     
284:     **Impact:**
285:     - Generated commands unusable
286:     - User must manually edit reason strings
287:     - Learning loop broken for sparse gaps
288:     
289:     **Fix:**

309:   - Summary:
310:     Anthropic restricts Opus 4.5 access via fingerprinting that blocks API usage but allows Claude Code CLI with Max subscription. This constraint forced a **dual spawn architecture**: primary path (OpenCode API + Sonnet/Flash, headless, high concurrency) and escape hatch (Claude CLI + Opus, tmux, crash-resistant). The escape hatch exists because critical infrastructure work (fixing the spawn system itself) can't depend on what might fail. Model choice now encodes reliability requirements, not just quality preferences.
311:     
312:     ---
313:   - Critical Invariants:
314:     1. **Never spawn OpenCode infrastructure work without --backend claude --tmux**
315:        - Violation: Agent kills itself mid-execution when server restarts
316:     
317:     2. **Infrastructure detection runs before model auto-selection**
318:        - Priority 2.5 (between explicit flags and model-based selection)
319:        - Ensures auto-apply happens even without explicit --backend
320:     
321:     3. **Opus only accessible via Claude CLI backend**
322:        - API requests to Opus fail with auth error
323:        - Fingerprinting checks more than headers (TLS, HTTP/2 frames, ordering)
324:     
325:     4. **Escape hatch provides true independence**
326:        - Claude CLI binary ≠ OpenCode server
327:        - Tmux session persists across service restarts
328:        - Different authentication path (Max subscription OAuth)
329:     
330:     ---
331:   - Why This Fails:
332:     ### Failure Mode 1: Zombie Agents (Jan 8, 2026)
333:     
334:     **Symptom:** Agents tracked in registry but never actually ran
335:     
336:     **Root Cause:** Spawning with `--model opus` before understanding auth gate
337:     - orch created registry entry
338:     - OpenCode session created
339:     - Anthropic rejected API request (fingerprinting)
340:     - Agent hung in "running" state
341:     - Consumed concurrency slot without doing work
342:     
343:     **Examples:**
344:     - orch-go-mo0ja, orch-go-pzi2i, orch-go-aoei0, orch-go-gd1gd, orch-go-lwc3o
345:     
346:     **Fix:** Never use `--model opus` without `--backend claude`
347:     
348:     ### Failure Mode 2: Header Injection Conflicts (Jan 8, 2026)
349:     
350:     **Symptom:** Gemini Flash spawns hung after attempting Opus bypass
351:     
352:     **Root Cause:** Injected Claude Code headers (`x-app: cli`, `anthropic-version`, etc.) into OpenCode's Anthropic provider
353:     - Bypassed Opus gate (didn't work)
354:     - Broke Gemini spawns (headers conflicted with Bun fetch/SDK)
355:     - System-wide impact from localized change
356:     
357:     **Lesson:** Fingerprinting is more sophisticated than headers alone
358:     
359:     ### Failure Mode 3: Infrastructure Work Kills Itself
360:     
361:     **Symptom:** Agent fixing OpenCode server crashes mid-execution
362:     
363:     **Root Cause:** Agent spawned via OpenCode API, agent's fix restarts OpenCode server, agent's session killed
364:     
365:     **Solution:** Infrastructure work detection auto-applies `--backend claude --tmux`
366:     
367:     **Why auto-detection matters:**
368:     - Humans forget to add flags manually
369:     - Task description might not mention "opencode" explicitly
370:     - Keyword scan catches common patterns
371:     - Escape hatch becomes invisible safety net

272: // InferSkillFromIssueType maps issue types to appropriate skills.
273: // Returns an error for types that cannot be spawned (e.g., epic) or unknown types.
274: //
275: // Bug handling: Defaults to "architect" (understand before fixing) rather than
276: // "systematic-debugging". This implements the "Premise Before Solution" principle -
277: // most bugs reported as vague symptoms need understanding before patching.
278: // Use explicit skill:systematic-debugging label for isolated bugs with clear cause.
279: func InferSkillFromIssueType(issueType string) (string, error) {
280: 	switch issueType {
281: 	case "bug":
282: 		// Default to architect: understand before fixing
283: 		// Use skill:systematic-debugging label for clear, isolated bugs
284: 		return "architect", nil
285: 	case "feature":
286: 		return "feature-impl", nil
287: 	case "task":
288: 		return "feature-impl", nil
289: 	case "investigation":
290: 		return "investigation", nil
291: 	case "epic":
292: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("cannot spawn work on epic issues - epics are decomposed into sub-issues")
293: 	case "":
294: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("issue type is empty")
295: 	default:
296: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("unknown issue type: %s", issueType)
297: 	}
298: }

300: // inferSkillFromBeadsIssue infers skill from a beads issue using labels, title, then type.
301: func inferSkillFromBeadsIssue(issue *beads.Issue) string {
302: 	// Check for skill:* labels first
303: 	for _, label := range issue.Labels {
304: 		if strings.HasPrefix(label, "skill:") {
305: 			return strings.TrimPrefix(label, "skill:")
306: 		}
307: 	}
308: 
309: 	// Check for title patterns (e.g., synthesis issues)
310: 	if strings.HasPrefix(issue.Title, "Synthesize ") && strings.Contains(issue.Title, " investigations") {
311: 		return "kb-reflect"
312: 	}
313: 
314: 	// Fall back to type-based inference
315: 	skill, err := InferSkillFromIssueType(issue.IssueType)
316: 	if err != nil {
317: 		return "feature-impl" // Default fallback
318: 	}
319: 	return skill
320: }
321: 
322: // inferMCPFromBeadsIssue extracts MCP server requirements from issue labels.
323: // Returns the MCP server name if found (e.g., "playwright" from "needs:playwright"),
324: // or empty string if no MCP-related label is present.
325: //
326: // This allows daemon-spawned agents to automatically get browser access when
327: // working on UI/CSS fixes that require visual verification.
328: func inferMCPFromBeadsIssue(issue *beads.Issue) string {
329: 	for _, label := range issue.Labels {
330: 		if strings.HasPrefix(label, "needs:") {
331: 			need := strings.TrimPrefix(label, "needs:")
332: 			// Map needs labels to MCP servers
333: 			switch need {
334: 			case "playwright":
335: 				return "playwright"
336: 				// Future: add more mappings as needed
337: 			}
338: 		}
339: 	}
340: 	return ""
341: }

343: func runWork(serverURL, beadsID string, inline bool) error {
344: 	// Get issue details from verify (for description)
345: 	issue, err := verify.GetIssue(beadsID)
346: 	if err != nil {
347: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get beads issue: %w", err)
348: 	}
349: 
350: 	// Infer skill and MCP from issue (labels, title pattern, then type)
351: 	// Use beads.Issue which has Labels for full skill/MCP inference
352: 	var skillName string
353: 	var mcpServer string
354: 	socketPath, connErr := beads.FindSocketPath("")
355: 	if connErr == nil {
356: 		beadsClient := beads.NewClient(socketPath)
357: 		if connErr := beadsClient.Connect(); connErr == nil {
358: 			defer beadsClient.Close()
359: 			beadsIssue, showErr := beadsClient.Show(beadsID)
360: 			if showErr == nil {
361: 				skillName = inferSkillFromBeadsIssue(beadsIssue)
362: 				mcpServer = inferMCPFromBeadsIssue(beadsIssue)
363: 			}
364: 		}
365: 	}
366: 	// Fall back to type-only inference if beads fails
367: 	if skillName == "" {
368: 		skillName, err = InferSkillFromIssueType(issue.IssueType)
369: 		if err != nil {
370: 			return fmt.Errorf("cannot work on issue %s: %w", beadsID, err)
371: 		}
372: 	}
373: 
374: 	// Use issue title and description as the task for full context
375: 	task := issue.Title
376: 	if issue.Description != "" {
377: 		task = issue.Title + "\n\n" + issue.Description
378: 	}
379: 
380: 	// Set the spawnIssue flag so runSpawnWithSkillInternal uses the existing issue
381: 	spawnIssue = beadsID
382: 
383: 	// Set the spawnMCP flag if the issue has a needs:* label (e.g., needs:playwright)
384: 	// This allows daemon-spawned agents to automatically get browser access for UI work
385: 	if mcpServer != "" {
386: 		spawnMCP = mcpServer
387: 	}
388: 
389: 	fmt.Printf("Starting work on: %s\n", beadsID)
390: 	fmt.Printf("  Title:  %s\n", issue.Title)
391: 	fmt.Printf("  Type:   %s\n", issue.IssueType)
392: 	fmt.Printf("  Skill:  %s\n", skillName)
393: 	if mcpServer != "" {
394: 		fmt.Printf("  MCP:    %s\n", mcpServer)
395: 	}
396: 
397: 	// Work command is daemon-driven (issue already created and triaged)
398: 	// Pass daemonDriven=true to skip triage bypass check
399: 	return runSpawnWithSkillInternal(serverURL, skillName, task, inline, true, false, false, true)
400: }

421: // checkAndAutoSwitchAccount checks if the current account is over usage thresholds
422: // and automatically switches to a better account if available.
423: // Returns nil if no switch was needed or switch succeeded.
424: // Logs the switch action if one occurs.
425: func checkAndAutoSwitchAccount() error {
426: 	// Get thresholds from environment or use defaults
427: 	thresholds := account.DefaultAutoSwitchThresholds()
428: 
429: 	// Allow override via environment variables
430: 	if envVal := os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_5H_THRESHOLD"); envVal != "" {
431: 		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(envVal, 64); err == nil && val > 0 && val <= 100 {
432: 			thresholds.FiveHourThreshold = val
433: 		}
434: 	}
435: 	if envVal := os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_WEEKLY_THRESHOLD"); envVal != "" {
436: 		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(envVal, 64); err == nil && val > 0 && val <= 100 {
437: 			thresholds.WeeklyThreshold = val
438: 		}
439: 	}
440: 	if envVal := os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_MIN_DELTA"); envVal != "" {
441: 		if val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(envVal, 64); err == nil && val >= 0 {
442: 			thresholds.MinHeadroomDelta = val
443: 		}
444: 	}
445: 
446: 	// Check if auto-switch is explicitly disabled
447: 	if os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_DISABLED") == "1" || os.Getenv("ORCH_AUTO_SWITCH_DISABLED") == "true" {
448: 		return nil
449: 	}
450: 
451: 	result, err := account.AutoSwitchIfNeeded(thresholds)
452: 	if err != nil {
453: 		// Log warning but don't block spawn - continue with current account
454: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: auto-switch check failed: %v\n", err)
455: 
456: 		// Check if the underlying error is a TokenRefreshError and provide guidance
457: 		var tokenErr *account.TokenRefreshError
458: 		if errors.As(err, &tokenErr) {
459: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  → %s\n", tokenErr.ActionableGuidance())
460: 		}
461: 		return nil
462: 	}
463: 
464: 	if result.Switched {
465: 		// Log the switch
466: 		logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
467: 		event := events.Event{
468: 			Type:      "account.auto_switched",
469: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
470: 			Data: map[string]interface{}{
471: 				"from_account":     result.FromAccount,
472: 				"to_account":       result.ToAccount,
473: 				"reason":           result.Reason,
474: 				"from_5h_used":     result.FromCapacity.FiveHourUsed,
475: 				"from_weekly_used": result.FromCapacity.SevenDayUsed,
476: 				"to_5h_used":       result.ToCapacity.FiveHourUsed,
477: 				"to_weekly_used":   result.ToCapacity.SevenDayUsed,
478: 			},

480: 		if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
481: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log account switch: %v\n", err)
482: 		}
483: 
484: 		fmt.Printf("🔄 Auto-switched account: %s\n", result.Reason)

487: 	return nil

506: // runPreFlightChecks performs all pre-spawn validation checks.
507: // Returns usage check result for telemetry, or error if any check fails.
508: func runPreFlightChecks(input *SpawnInput, preCheckDir string) (*gates.UsageCheckResult, error) {
509: 	// Check for --bypass-triage flag (required for manual spawns)
510: 	// Daemon-driven spawns skip this check (issue already triaged)
511: 	if err := gates.CheckTriageBypass(input.DaemonDriven, spawnBypassTriage, input.SkillName, input.Task); err != nil {
512: 		return nil, err
513: 	}
514: 
515: 	// Log the triage bypass for Phase 2 review (only for manual bypasses, not daemon-driven)
516: 	if !input.DaemonDriven && spawnBypassTriage {
517: 		gates.LogTriageBypass(input.SkillName, input.Task)
518: 	}
519: 
520: 	// Check verification gate (Phase 3: Session Continuity Gate)
521: 	// Block spawn if unverified Tier 1 work exists (prevents cascade pattern)
522: 	// Independent parallel work can use --bypass-verification to override
523: 	if err := gates.CheckVerificationGate(spawnBypassVerification, spawnBypassReason); err != nil {
524: 		return nil, err
525: 	}
526: 
527: 	// Check concurrency limit before spawning
528: 	if err := gates.CheckConcurrency(input.ServerURL, spawnMaxAgents, extractBeadsIDFromTitle); err != nil {
529: 		return nil, err
530: 	}
531: 
532: 	// Proactive rate limit monitoring: warn at 80%, block at 95%
533: 	usageCheckResult, usageErr := gates.CheckRateLimit()
534: 	if usageErr != nil {
535: 		// usageErr contains formatted blocking message
536: 		return nil, usageErr
537: 	}
538: 
539: 	// STRATEGIC-FIRST ORCHESTRATION: Check for hotspots in task target area
540: 	// In hotspot areas (5+ bugs, persistent failures), strategic approach is recommended
541: 	// Warning shown but spawn proceeds (non-blocking)
542: 	gates.CheckHotspot(preCheckDir, input.Task, input.SkillName, input.DaemonDriven, func(dir, t string) (*gates.HotspotResult, error) {
543: 		result, err := RunHotspotCheckForSpawn(dir, t)
544: 		if err != nil || result == nil {
545: 			return nil, err
546: 		}
547: 		return &gates.HotspotResult{HasHotspots: result.HasHotspots, Warning: result.Warning}, nil
548: 	})
549: 
550: 	return usageCheckResult, nil
551: }

553: // resolveProjectDirectory determines the project directory and name.
554: // Uses --workdir if provided, otherwise current working directory.
555: func resolveProjectDirectory() (projectDir, projectName string, err error) {
556: 	if spawnWorkdir != "" {
557: 		// User specified target directory via --workdir
558: 		projectDir, err = filepath.Abs(spawnWorkdir)
559: 		if err != nil {
560: 			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve workdir path: %w", err)
561: 		}
562: 		// Verify directory exists
563: 		if stat, err := os.Stat(projectDir); err != nil {
564: 			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("workdir does not exist: %s", projectDir)
565: 		} else if !stat.IsDir() {
566: 			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("workdir is not a directory: %s", projectDir)
567: 		}
568: 	} else {
569: 		// Default: use current working directory
570: 		projectDir, err = os.Getwd()
571: 		if err != nil {
572: 			return "", "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current directory: %w", err)
573: 		}
574: 	}
575: 
576: 	// Get project name from directory
577: 	projectName = filepath.Base(projectDir)
578: 	return projectDir, projectName, nil
579: }

581: // loadSkillAndGenerateWorkspace loads skill content and generates workspace name.
582: // Also ensures orch scaffolding is initialized.
583: func loadSkillAndGenerateWorkspace(skillName, projectName, task, projectDir string) (
584: 	skillContent, workspaceName string,
585: 	isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator bool,
586: 	err error) {
587: 
588: 	// Check and optionally auto-initialize scaffolding
589: 	if err := ensureOrchScaffolding(projectDir, spawnAutoInit, spawnNoTrack); err != nil {
590: 		return "", "", false, false, err
591: 	}
592: 
593: 	// Load skill content with dependencies (e.g., worker-base patterns)
594: 	loader := skills.DefaultLoader()
595: 
596: 	// First load raw skill content (without dependencies) to detect skill type
597: 	// This is needed because LoadSkillWithDependencies puts dependency content first,
598: 	// which means the main skill's frontmatter isn't at the start of the combined content
599: 	rawSkillContent, err := loader.LoadSkillContent(skillName)
600: 	if err == nil {
601: 		if metadata, err := skills.ParseSkillMetadata(rawSkillContent); err == nil {
602: 			isOrchestrator = metadata.SkillType == "policy" || metadata.SkillType == "orchestrator"
603: 		}
604: 	}
605: 	// Detect meta-orchestrator by skill name (not skill-type)
606: 	// This enables tiered context templates for different orchestrator levels
607: 	if skillName == "meta-orchestrator" {
608: 		isMetaOrchestrator = true
609: 	}
610: 
611: 	// Generate workspace name
612: 	// Meta-orchestrators use "meta-" prefix instead of project prefix for visual distinction
613: 	// Orchestrators use "orch" skill prefix instead of "work" for visual distinction from workers
614: 	workspaceName = spawn.GenerateWorkspaceName(projectName, skillName, task, spawn.WorkspaceNameOptions{
615: 		IsMetaOrchestrator: isMetaOrchestrator,
616: 		IsOrchestrator:     isOrchestrator,
617: 	})
618: 
619: 	// Now load full skill content with dependencies for the actual spawn
620: 	skillContent, err = loader.LoadSkillWithDependencies(skillName)
621: 	if err != nil {
622: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: could not load skill '%s': %v\n", skillName, err)
623: 		skillContent = "" // Continue without skill content
624: 	}
625: 
626: 	return skillContent, workspaceName, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator, nil
627: }

629: // setupBeadsTracking determines beads ID and manages issue lifecycle.
630: // Returns final beads ID (empty if untracked), or error if setup fails.
631: func setupBeadsTracking(skillName, task, projectName, beadsIssueFlag string, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator bool, serverURL string) (string, error) {
632: 	// Determine beads ID - either from flag, create new issue, or skip if --no-track
633: 	// Orchestrators skip beads tracking entirely - they're interactive sessions with Dylan,
634: 	// not autonomous tasks. SESSION_HANDOFF.md is richer than beads comments.
635: 	skipBeadsForOrchestrator := isOrchestrator || isMetaOrchestrator
636: 	beadsID, err := determineBeadsID(projectName, skillName, task, beadsIssueFlag, spawnNoTrack || skipBeadsForOrchestrator, createBeadsIssue)
637: 	if err != nil {
638: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to determine beads ID: %w", err)
639: 	}
640: 	if skipBeadsForOrchestrator {
641: 		fmt.Println("Skipping beads tracking (orchestrator session)")
642: 	} else if spawnNoTrack {
643: 		fmt.Println("Skipping beads tracking (--no-track)")
644: 	}
645: 
646: 	// Check for retry patterns on existing issues - surface to prevent blind respawning
647: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
648: 	if !spawnNoTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
649: 		if stats, err := verify.GetFixAttemptStats(beadsID); err == nil && stats.IsRetryPattern() {
650: 			warning := verify.FormatRetryWarning(stats)
651: 			if warning != "" {
652: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n%s\n", warning)
653: 			}
654: 		}
655: 	}
656: 
657: 	// DISABLED: Dependency check gate (Jan 4, 2026)
658: 	// This was added to prevent spawning on issues with unresolved dependencies,
659: 	// but it added friction without clear benefit. Dependencies are informational,
660: 	// not blocking - agents can often make progress even if dependencies exist.
661: 	// See: .kb/post-mortems/2026-01-02-system-spiral-dec27-jan02.md
662: 	/*
663: 		if !spawnNoTrack && beadsIssueFlag != "" && !spawnForce {
664: 			blockers, err := beads.CheckBlockingDependencies(beadsID)
665: 			// ... gate logic disabled ...
666: 		}
667: 	*/
668: 	_ = spawnForce // silence unused variable warning (flag still exists but doesn't gate)
669: 
670: 	// Check if issue is already being worked on (prevent duplicate spawns)
671: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
672: 	if !spawnNoTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
673: 		if issue, err := verify.GetIssue(beadsID); err == nil {
674: 			if issue.Status == "closed" {
675: 				return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s is already closed", beadsID)
676: 			}
677: 			if issue.Status == "in_progress" {
678: 				// Check if there's a truly active agent for this issue
679: 				// OpenCode persists sessions to disk, so we must verify liveness not just existence
680: 				client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
681: 				sessions, _ := client.ListSessions("")
682: 				for _, s := range sessions {
683: 					if strings.Contains(s.Title, beadsID) {
684: 						// Session exists - but is it actually active (recently updated)?
685: 						// Use 30 minute threshold - if no activity, session is stale
686: 						if client.IsSessionActive(s.ID, 30*time.Minute) {
687: 							return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s is already in_progress with active agent (session %s). Use 'orch send %s' to interact or 'orch abandon %s' to restart", beadsID, s.ID, s.ID, beadsID)
688: 						}
689: 						// Session exists but is stale - log and continue (allow respawn)
690: 						fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Note: found stale session %s for issue %s (no activity in 30m)\n", s.ID[:12], beadsID)
691: 					}
692: 				}
693: 				// No active session - check if Phase: Complete was reported
694: 				// If so, orchestrator needs to run 'orch complete' before respawning
695: 				if complete, err := verify.IsPhaseComplete(beadsID); err == nil && complete {
696: 					return "", fmt.Errorf("issue %s has Phase: Complete but is not closed. Run 'orch complete %s' first", beadsID, beadsID)
697: 				}
698: 				// In progress but no active agent and not Phase: Complete - warn but allow respawn
699: 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: issue %s is in_progress but no active agent found. Respawning.\n", beadsID)
700: 			}
701: 		}
702: 	}
703: 
704: 	// Update beads issue status to in_progress (only if tracking a real issue)
705: 	// Skip for orchestrators since they don't use beads tracking
706: 	if !spawnNoTrack && !skipBeadsForOrchestrator && beadsIssueFlag != "" {
707: 		if err := verify.UpdateIssueStatus(beadsID, "in_progress"); err != nil {
708: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update beads issue status: %v\n", err)
709: 			// Continue anyway
710: 		}
711: 	}
712: 
713: 	return beadsID, nil
714: }

741: // gatherSpawnContext gathers KB context and performs gap analysis.
742: // Returns context string, gap analysis, model injection info, or error.
743: func gatherSpawnContext(skillContent, task, beadsID, projectDir string) (
744: 	kbContext string,
745: 	gapAnalysis *spawn.GapAnalysis,
746: 	hasInjectedModels bool,
747: 	primaryModelPath string,
748: 	err error) {
749: 
750: 	if spawnSkipArtifactCheck {
751: 		fmt.Println("Skipping context check (--skip-artifact-check)")
752: 		return "", nil, false, "", nil
753: 	}
754: 
755: 	// Parse skill requirements to determine what context to gather
756: 	requires := spawn.ParseSkillRequires(skillContent)
757: 
758: 	if requires != nil && requires.HasRequirements() {
759: 		// Skill-driven context gathering
760: 		fmt.Printf("Gathering context (skill requires: %s)\n", requires.String())
761: 		kbContext = spawn.GatherRequiredContext(requires, task, beadsID, projectDir)
762: 		// For skill-driven context, create a basic gap analysis from the results
763: 		// This is a placeholder - skills may provide their own gap info
764: 		gapAnalysis = spawn.AnalyzeGaps(nil, task)
765: 	} else {
766: 		// Fall back to default kb context check with full gap analysis
767: 		gapResult := runPreSpawnKBCheckFull(task)
768: 		kbContext = gapResult.Context
769: 		gapAnalysis = gapResult.GapAnalysis
770: 
771: 		// Extract model injection info for probe vs investigation routing
772: 		if gapResult.FormatResult != nil {
773: 			hasInjectedModels = gapResult.FormatResult.HasInjectedModels
774: 			if hasInjectedModels {
775: 				// Extract primary model path from KB context result
776: 				primaryModelPath = extractPrimaryModelPath(gapResult.FormatResult)
777: 			}
778: 		}
779: 	}
780: 
781: 	// Check gap gating - may block spawn if context quality is too low
782: 	if err := checkGapGating(gapAnalysis, spawnGateOnGap, spawnSkipGapGate, spawnGapThreshold); err != nil {
783: 		return "", nil, false, "", err
784: 	}
785: 
786: 	// Record gap for learning loop (if gaps detected)
787: 	if gapAnalysis != nil && gapAnalysis.HasGaps {
788: 		recordGapForLearning(gapAnalysis, skillContent, task)
789: 	}
790: 
791: 	// Log if skip-gap-gate was used (documents conscious bypass)
792: 	if spawnSkipGapGate && gapAnalysis != nil && gapAnalysis.ShouldBlockSpawn(spawnGapThreshold) {
793: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  Bypassing gap gate (--skip-gap-gate): context quality %d\n", gapAnalysis.ContextQuality)
794: 		// Log the bypass for pattern detection
795: 		logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
796: 		event := events.Event{
797: 			Type:      "gap.gate.bypassed",
798: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
799: 			Data: map[string]interface{}{
800: 				"task":            task,
801: 				"context_quality": gapAnalysis.ContextQuality,
802: 				"beads_id":        beadsID,
803: 				"skill":           skillContent,
804: 			},
805: 		}
806: 		if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
807: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log gap bypass: %v\n", err)
808: 		}
809: 	}
810: 
811: 	return kbContext, gapAnalysis, hasInjectedModels, primaryModelPath, nil
812: }

847: // determineSpawnBackend determines spawn backend with auto-selection.
848: // Priority: --backend flag > --opus flag > infrastructure detection > model-based > config > default.
849: func determineSpawnBackend(resolvedModel model.ModelSpec, task, beadsID, projectDir string) (string, error) {
850: 	// Load project config (used for backend default)
851: 	projCfg, _ := config.Load(projectDir)
852: 
853: 	// Default to claude (Max subscription covers Claude CLI usage)
854: 	backend := "claude"
855: 
856: 	if spawnBackendFlag != "" {
857: 		// Explicit --backend flag: highest priority
858: 		backend = spawnBackendFlag
859: 		// Validate backend value
860: 		if backend != "claude" && backend != "opencode" {
861: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid --backend value: %s (must be 'claude' or 'opencode')", backend)
862: 		}
863: 	} else if spawnOpus {
864: 		// Explicit --opus flag: use claude CLI
865: 		backend = "claude"
866: 	} else if isInfrastructureWork(task, beadsID) {
867: 		// Infrastructure work detection: auto-apply escape hatch
868: 		// Agents working on OpenCode/orch infrastructure need claude backend + tmux
869: 		// to survive server restarts (prevent agents from killing themselves)
870: 		backend = "claude"
871: 		fmt.Println("🔧 Infrastructure work detected - auto-applying escape hatch (--backend claude --tmux)")
872: 		fmt.Println("   This ensures the agent survives OpenCode server restarts.")
873: 
874: 		// Log the infrastructure work detection for pattern analysis
875: 		logger := events.NewLogger(events.DefaultLogPath())
876: 		event := events.Event{
877: 			Type:      "spawn.infrastructure_detected",
878: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
879: 			Data: map[string]interface{}{
880: 				"task":     task,
881: 				"beads_id": beadsID,
882: 				"skill":    "", // Will be filled by caller
883: 			},
884: 		}
885: 		if err := logger.Log(event); err != nil {
886: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log infrastructure detection: %v\n", err)
887: 		}
888: 	} else if spawnModel != "" {
889: 		// Auto-select backend based on model
890: 		modelLower := strings.ToLower(spawnModel)
891: 		if modelLower == "opus" || strings.Contains(modelLower, "opus") {
892: 			// Opus model: use claude CLI (Max subscription)
893: 			backend = "claude"
894: 			fmt.Println("Auto-selected claude backend for opus model")
895: 		} else if modelLower == "sonnet" || strings.Contains(modelLower, "sonnet") {
896: 			// Sonnet model: use opencode (pay-per-token API)
897: 			backend = "opencode"
898: 		}
899: 		// Other models keep the default backend (claude)
900: 	} else if projCfg != nil && projCfg.SpawnMode == "claude" {
901: 		// Config default: respect project spawn_mode setting
902: 		backend = "claude"
903: 	}
904: 
905: 	// Validate mode+model combination
906: 	if err := validateModeModelCombo(backend, resolvedModel); err != nil {
907: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "⚠️  %v\n", err)
908: 	}
909: 
910: 	return backend, nil
911: }

960: 		DesignWorkspace:    spawnDesignWorkspace,
961: 		DesignMockupPath:   ctx.DesignMockupPath,
962: 		DesignPromptPath:   ctx.DesignPromptPath,
963: 		DesignNotes:        ctx.DesignNotes,
964: 	}
965: }
966: 
967: // validateAndWriteContext validates context size, writes SPAWN_CONTEXT.md, and generates prompt.
968: // Returns minimal prompt, or error if validation fails.
969: func validateAndWriteContext(cfg *spawn.Config) (minimalPrompt string, err error) {
970: 	// Pre-spawn token estimation and validation
971: 	if err := spawn.ValidateContextSize(cfg); err != nil {
972: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("pre-spawn validation failed: %w", err)
973: 	}
974: 
975: 	// Warn about large contexts (but don't block)
976: 	if shouldWarn, warning := spawn.ShouldWarnAboutSize(cfg); shouldWarn {
977: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "%s", warning)
978: 	}
979: 
980: 	// Check for existing workspace before writing context
981: 	// This prevents accidentally overwriting SESSION_HANDOFF.md from completed sessions
982: 	// Note: With unique suffixes in workspace names (since Jan 2026), collisions are rare
983: 	// but this provides an extra safety net and meaningful error messages
984: 	if err := checkWorkspaceExists(cfg.WorkspacePath(), spawnForce); err != nil {
985: 		return "", err
986: 	}
987: 
988: 	// Write SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
989: 	if err := spawn.WriteContext(cfg); err != nil {
990: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write spawn context: %w", err)
991: 	}
992: 
993: 	// Record spawn in session (if session is active)
994: 	if sessionStore, err := session.New(""); err == nil {
995: 		if err := sessionStore.RecordSpawn(cfg.BeadsID, cfg.SkillName, cfg.Task, cfg.ProjectDir); err != nil {
996: 			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to record spawn in session: %v\n", err)
997: 		}
998: 	}
999: 
1000: 	// Generate minimal prompt
1001: 	minimalPrompt = spawn.MinimalPrompt(cfg)
1002: 	return minimalPrompt, nil
1003: }
1004: 
1005: // dispatchSpawn routes to the appropriate spawn mode function.
1006: // Handles inline, headless, claude, and tmux modes.
1007: func dispatchSpawn(input *SpawnInput, cfg *spawn.Config, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task, serverURL string) error {
1008: 	// Spawn mode: inline (blocking TUI), tmux (opt-in for workers, default for orchestrators), claude (tmux), or headless (default for workers)
1009: 	if input.Inline {
1010: 		// Inline mode (blocking) - run in current terminal with TUI
1011: 		return runSpawnInline(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
1012: 	}
1013: 
1014: 	// Explicit --headless flag overrides all other mode decisions
1015: 	if input.Headless {
1016: 		return runSpawnHeadless(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
1017: 	}
1018: 
1019: 	// Claude mode: Use tmux + claude CLI
1020: 	if cfg.SpawnMode == "claude" {
1021: 		return runSpawnClaude(serverURL, cfg, beadsID, skillName, task, input.Attach)
1022: 	}
1023: 
1024: 	// Orchestrator-type skills default to tmux mode (visible interaction)
1025: 	// Workers default to headless mode (automation-friendly)
1026: 	useTmux := input.Tmux || input.Attach || cfg.IsOrchestrator
1027: 	if useTmux {
1028: 		// Tmux mode - visible, interruptible
1029: 		// Default for orchestrator skills, opt-in for workers
1030: 		return runSpawnTmux(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task, input.Attach)
1031: 	}
1032: 
1033: 	// Default for workers: Headless mode - spawn via HTTP API (automation-friendly, no TUI overhead)
1034: 	return runSpawnHeadless(serverURL, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task)
1035: }
1036: 
1037: func runSpawnWithSkill(serverURL, skillName, task string, inline bool, headless bool, tmux bool, attach bool) error {
1038: 	return runSpawnWithSkillInternal(serverURL, skillName, task, inline, headless, tmux, attach, false)
1039: }
1040: 
1041: // runSpawnWithSkillInternal is the internal implementation that supports daemon-driven spawns.
1042: // When daemonDriven is true, the triage bypass check is skipped (issue already triaged).
1043: // runSpawnWithSkillInternal is the main spawn pipeline following complete_cmd.go pattern.
1044: // Extracted into helper functions for better readability and maintainability.
1045: func runSpawnWithSkillInternal(serverURL, skillName, task string, inline bool, headless bool, tmux bool, attach bool, daemonDriven bool) error {
1046: 	// Build input parameter struct
1047: 	input := &SpawnInput{
1048: 		ServerURL:    serverURL,
1049: 		SkillName:    skillName,
1050: 		Task:         task,
1051: 		Inline:       inline,
1052: 		Headless:     headless,
1053: 		Tmux:         tmux,
1054: 		Attach:       attach,
1055: 		DaemonDriven: daemonDriven,
1056: 	}
1057: 
1058: 	// Get project directory early for pre-flight checks
1059: 	var preCheckDir string
1060: 	if spawnWorkdir != "" {
1061: 		if absPath, err := filepath.Abs(spawnWorkdir); err == nil {
1062: 			preCheckDir = absPath
1063: 		}
1064: 	} else {
1065: 		preCheckDir, _ = os.Getwd()
1066: 	}
1067: 
1068: 	// 1. Pre-flight checks
1069: 	usageCheckResult, err := runPreFlightChecks(input, preCheckDir)
1070: 	if err != nil {
1071: 		return err
1072: 	}
1073: 
1074: 	// 2. Resolve project directory
1075: 	projectDir, projectName, err := resolveProjectDirectory()
1076: 	if err != nil {
1077: 		return err
1078: 	}
1079: 
1080: 	// 3. Load skill and generate workspace
1081: 	skillContent, workspaceName, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator, err := loadSkillAndGenerateWorkspace(skillName, projectName, task, projectDir)
1082: 	if err != nil {
1083: 		return err
1084: 	}
1085: 
1086: 	// 4. Setup beads tracking
1087: 	beadsID, err := setupBeadsTracking(skillName, task, projectName, spawnIssue, isOrchestrator, isMetaOrchestrator, serverURL)
1088: 	if err != nil {
1089: 		return err
1090: 	}
1091: 
1092: 	// 5. Resolve and validate model
1093: 	resolvedModel, err := resolveAndValidateModel(spawnModel)
1094: 	if err != nil {
1095: 		return err
1096: 	}
1097: 
1098: 	// 6. Gather spawn context
1099: 	kbContext, gapAnalysis, hasInjectedModels, primaryModelPath, err := gatherSpawnContext(skillContent, task, beadsID, projectDir)
1100: 	if err != nil {
1101: 		return err
1102: 	}
1103: 
1104: 	// 7. Extract bug reproduction info
1105: 	isBug, reproSteps := extractBugReproInfo(beadsID, spawnNoTrack || isOrchestrator || isMetaOrchestrator)
1106: 
1107: 	// 8. Build usage info
1108: 	usageInfo := buildUsageInfo(usageCheckResult)
1109: 
1110: 	// 9. Determine spawn backend
1111: 	spawnBackend, err := determineSpawnBackend(resolvedModel, task, beadsID, projectDir)
1112: 	if err != nil {
1113: 		return err
1114: 	}
1115: 
1116: 	// 10. Load design artifacts
1117: 	designMockupPath, designPromptPath, designNotes := loadDesignArtifacts(spawnDesignWorkspace, projectDir)
1118: 
1119: 	// 11. Build spawn context
1120: 	ctx := &SpawnContext{
1121: 		Task:               task,
1122: 		SkillName:          skillName,
1123: 		ProjectDir:         projectDir,
1124: 		ProjectName:        projectName,
1125: 		WorkspaceName:      workspaceName,
1126: 		SkillContent:       skillContent,
1127: 		BeadsID:            beadsID,
1128: 		IsOrchestrator:     isOrchestrator,
1129: 		IsMetaOrchestrator: isMetaOrchestrator,
1130: 		ResolvedModel:      resolvedModel,
1131: 		KBContext:          kbContext,
1132: 		GapAnalysis:        gapAnalysis,
1133: 		HasInjectedModels:  hasInjectedModels,
1134: 		PrimaryModelPath:   primaryModelPath,
1135: 		IsBug:              isBug,
1136: 		ReproSteps:         reproSteps,
1137: 		UsageInfo:          usageInfo,
1138: 		SpawnBackend:       spawnBackend,
1139: 		Tier:               determineSpawnTier(skillName, spawnLight, spawnFull),
1140: 		DesignMockupPath:   designMockupPath,
1141: 		DesignPromptPath:   designPromptPath,
1142: 		DesignNotes:        designNotes,
1143: 	}
1144: 
1145: 	// 12. Build spawn config
1146: 	cfg := buildSpawnConfig(ctx)
1147: 
1148: 	// 13. Validate and write context
1149: 	minimalPrompt, err := validateAndWriteContext(cfg)
1150: 	if err != nil {
1151: 		return err
1152: 	}
1153: 
1154: 	// 14. Dispatch spawn
1155: 	return dispatchSpawn(input, cfg, minimalPrompt, beadsID, skillName, task, serverURL)
1156: }
1157: 
1158: // formatSessionTitle formats the session title to include beads ID for matching.
1159: // Format: "workspace-name [beads-id]" (e.g., "og-debug-orch-status-23dec [orch-go-v4mw]")
1160: // This allows extractBeadsIDFromTitle to find agents in orch status.
1161: func formatSessionTitle(workspaceName, beadsID string) string {
1162: 	if beadsID == "" {
1163: 		return workspaceName
1164: 	}
1165: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s [%s]", workspaceName, beadsID)
1166: }

1168: // registerOrchestratorSession registers an orchestrator session in the session registry.
1169: // This is called after successful spawn for orchestrator-type skills.
1170: // Workers do not use the session registry - they use beads for lifecycle tracking.
1171: func registerOrchestratorSession(cfg *spawn.Config, sessionID, task string) {
1172: 	if !cfg.IsOrchestrator && !cfg.IsMetaOrchestrator {
1173: 		return // Only register orchestrator sessions

1186: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to register orchestrator session: %v\n", err)
1187: 	}
1188: }

<content>1: # Code Extraction Patterns
2: 
3: **Purpose:** Single authoritative reference for extracting code into separate files during refactoring. Read this before starting extraction work.
4: 
5: **Last verified:** 2026-01-08
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Overview
10: 
11: This guide synthesizes learnings from 13 extraction investigations covering Go packages (serve.go, main.go), Svelte components (+page.svelte, agent-detail panel tabs), and TypeScript services (SSE connection management). It provides proven patterns and gotchas for safely splitting large files.
12: 
13: ---
14: 
15: ## Architecture
16: 
17: ```
18: Before Extraction:           After Extraction:
19: ┌─────────────────────┐      ┌─────────────────────┐
20: │  monolithic.go      │      │  main.go (setup)    │
21: │  - shared utilities │      ├─────────────────────┤
22: │  - domain A code    │  →   │  shared.go          │
23: │  - domain B code    │      ├─────────────────────┤
24: │  - domain C code    │      │  domain_a_cmd.go    │
25: │  (~3000+ lines)     │      │  domain_b_cmd.go    │
26: └─────────────────────┘      │  domain_c_cmd.go    │
27:                              │  (~300-800 lines each)│
28:                              └─────────────────────┘
29: 
30: Extraction Order:
31: 1. Shared utilities FIRST (breaks cross-dependencies)
32: 2. Domain files in parallel (independent units)
33: ```
34: 
35: ---
36: 
37: ## How It Works
38: 
39: ### Phase 1: Extract Shared Utilities First
40: 
41: **What:** Move cross-command/cross-handler functions to a dedicated shared.go file.
42: 
43: **Key insight:** Shared utilities MUST be extracted first. If you extract domain files first, you'll duplicate shared code or create complex import chains.
44: 
45: | Aspect | Details |
46: |--------|---------|
47: | Target file | `shared.go` for Go, `lib/utils/` for Svelte |
48: | Content | Functions used by 2+ domains (e.g., truncate, findWorkspaceByID) |
49: | Line reduction | Usually 200-300 lines from source |
50: 
51: ### Phase 2: Extract Domain-Specific Code
52: 
53: **What:** Move command handlers and their helpers to dedicated files.
54: 
55: **Key insight:** Extract the ENTIRE domain unit together: command definition + flags + init() + run function + types + helpers. Partial extraction creates confusion.
56: 
57: | Naming Convention | Example |
58: |-------------------|---------|
59: | Go commands | `status_cmd.go`, `clean_cmd.go`, `send_cmd.go` |
60: | Go handlers | `serve_agents.go`, `serve_beads.go`, `serve_system.go` |
61: | Svelte components | `lib/components/stats-bar/stats-bar.svelte` |
62: 
63: ### Phase 3: Extract Sub-Domains (If Needed)
64: 
65: **What:** If a domain file grows too large (>800 lines), split further by responsibility.
66: 
67: **Key insight:** Use descriptive suffixes: `serve_agents_cache.go` (cache infrastructure), `serve_agents_events.go` (SSE event handlers).
68: 
69: ### Phase 4: Extract Feature Tabs (Svelte)
70: 
71: **What:** Extract tab/section content from large panel components into dedicated sub-components within the same directory.
72: 
73: **Key insight:** Unlike new component extraction (which creates new directories with barrel exports), feature tab extraction adds files to an existing component directory. Props-based design with `$props()` rune keeps interface clean.
74: 
75: | Aspect | Details |
76: |--------|---------|
77: | Pattern | Large panel → multiple tab components |
78: | Example | `agent-detail-panel.svelte` → `ActivityTab.svelte`, `SynthesisTab.svelte` |
79: | Interface | Single `agent: Agent` prop, self-contained state with `$state()` |
80: | Exports | Add to existing `index.ts` barrel export |
81: | Line reduction | ~200-230 lines per tab |
82: 
83: **When to use:** Panel component exceeds 400 lines with multiple logical sections (tabs, accordions, distinct UI regions).
84: 
85: ### Phase 5: Extract Shared Services (TypeScript)
86: 
87: **What:** Extract duplicate infrastructure patterns (SSE connections, API clients, state management) into shared services.
88: 
89: **Key insight:** Look for the same connection/lifecycle patterns repeated across multiple stores. Extract the infrastructure, keep domain logic in stores.
90: 
91: | Aspect | Details |
92: |--------|---------|
93: | Pattern | Duplicate infrastructure → shared service factory |
94: | Example | SSE connection logic in `agents.ts` + `agentlog.ts` → `lib/services/sse-connection.ts` |
95: | Interface | Factory function with callbacks for domain-specific handling |
96: | Benefits | Centralized lifecycle management, single fix point for bugs |
97: | Line reduction | ~70+ lines per consumer |
98: 
99: **When to use:** Same infrastructure pattern (EventSource lifecycle, reconnection, timers) appears in 2+ stores.
100: 
101: ---
102: 
103: ## Key Concepts
104: 
105: | Concept | Definition | Why It Matters |
106: |---------|------------|----------------|
107: | Package-level visibility | All Go files in same package share function visibility | No imports needed between `main.go` and `status_cmd.go` |
108: | Cohesive extraction unit | All related code for one concern | Keep command + helpers + types together |
109: | Test file co-location | Tests move with their source | `status_cmd.go` → `status_cmd_test.go` |
110: | Svelte bindable props | `$bindable` for two-way parent-child binding | Enables clean component extraction with state sharing |
111: 
112: ---
113: 
114: ## Common Problems
115: 
116: ### "Redeclaration error after extraction"
117: 
118: **Cause:** Code exists in both old and new files.
119: 
120: **Fix:** 
121: 1. Remove extracted code from the source file after creating the new file
122: 2. Run `go build ./cmd/orch/` to verify no duplicates
123: 
124: **NOT the fix:** Creating the new file without removing from old file
125: 
126: ### "Parallel agent already completed the extraction"
127: 
128: **Cause:** Multiple agents working on related tasks can race.
129: 
130: **This is expected behavior.** When extraction work overlaps:
131: 
132: **Fix options:**
133: 1. Check `git log --oneline -5` at task start to detect prior completion
134: 2. If already done, verify correctness and close as "already completed"
135: 3. If partial, fix remaining conflicts (usually duplicate test functions)
136: 
137: ### "Test function redeclared in this block"
138: 
139: **Cause:** Tests exist in both old test file and new test file after extraction.
140: 
141: **Fix:**
142: 1. Identify which test file should own each test
143: 2. Remove duplicates from the wrong file
144: 3. Follow pattern: tests stay with their handlers
145: 
146: ### "Import not used after extraction"
147: 
148: **Cause:** Imports remain in source file after code is moved out.
149: 
150: **Fix:** Remove unused imports from source file. Go compiler will tell you which ones.
151: 
152: ---
153: 
154: ## Key Decisions (from investigations)
155: 
156: These are settled. Don't re-investigate:
157: 
158: - **Extract shared utilities first** - Prevents duplication and complex imports
159: - **Keep all related code together** - Command + flags + init + run + types in one file
160: - **Tests follow handlers** - serve_agents_test.go moves with serve_agents.go
161: - **Target ~300-800 lines per file** - Larger files should be split further
162: 
163: ---
164: 
165: ## What Lives Where
166: 
167: | File Type | Location | Purpose |
168: |-----------|----------|---------|
169: | Shared Go utilities | `cmd/orch/shared.go` | Cross-command helpers (truncate, findWorkspace, etc.) |
170: | Command files | `cmd/orch/{name}_cmd.go` | Single CLI command + all related code |
171: | Handler files | `cmd/orch/serve_{domain}.go` | HTTP handlers for one domain |
172: | Sub-domain files | `cmd/orch/serve_{domain}_{aspect}.go` | Split large handlers (cache, events) |
173: | Svelte components | `web/src/lib/components/{name}/` | Reusable UI components |
174: | Svelte feature tabs | `web/src/lib/components/{parent}/{name}-tab.svelte` | Sub-components within panel directories |
175: | TypeScript services | `web/src/lib/services/{name}.ts` | Shared infrastructure (SSE, API clients) |
176: 
177: ---
178: 
179: ## Extraction Workflow
180: 
181: ### For Go Files
182: 
183: ```bash
184: # 1. Identify extraction targets (shared utilities, then domains)
185: wc -l cmd/orch/main.go  # Check current size
186: 
187: # 2. Create new file with extracted code
188: # - Add package header
189: # - Copy types, functions, helpers
190: # - Add necessary imports
191: 
192: # 3. Remove extracted code from source file
193: # - Delete copied code
194: # - Clean up unused imports
195: 
196: # 4. Verify
197: go build ./cmd/orch/
198: go test ./cmd/orch/...
199: 
200: # 5. Move tests if applicable
201: # - Create {name}_test.go
202: # - Move related tests from old test file
203: # - Remove duplicates
204: ```
205: 
206: ### For Svelte Components (New Directory)
207: 
208: ```bash
209: # 1. Create component directory
210: mkdir -p web/src/lib/components/{name}
211: 
212: # 2. Create component file
213: # - Move template, script, style
214: # - Import stores directly
215: # - Use $bindable for two-way props
216: 
217: # 3. Create index.ts barrel export
218: echo "export { default as ComponentName } from './component-name.svelte';" > index.ts
219: 
220: # 4. Update parent
221: # - Import component
222: # - Replace inline code with <ComponentName />
223: 
224: # 5. Verify
225: # - Build: npm run build (or similar)
226: # - Visual: Check in browser
227: ```
228: 
229: ### For Svelte Feature Tabs (Within Existing Directory)
230: 
231: ```bash
232: # 1. Identify tab boundaries in parent component
233: # - Look for sections with clear responsibilities (Activity, Synthesis, etc.)
234: # - Each tab should be ~150-250 lines
235: 
236: # 2. Create tab component in same directory
237: # - File: web/src/lib/components/{parent}/{name}-tab.svelte
238: # - Use $props() rune: let { agent }: { agent: Agent } = $props()
239: # - Use $state() for local state (filters, toggles)
240: 
241: # 3. Add to existing barrel export
242: # - Update index.ts: export { default as NameTab } from './name-tab.svelte';
243: 
244: # 4. Replace inline section in parent
245: # - Import NameTab
246: # - Replace markup with <NameTab {agent} />
247: # - Remove duplicated helper functions
248: 
249: # 5. Verify
250: # - Build: cd web && bun run build
251: # - TypeScript: cd web && bun run check
252: # - Visual: Check in browser
253: ```
254: 
255: ### For TypeScript Services (Shared Infrastructure)
256: 
257: ```bash
258: # 1. Identify duplicate infrastructure patterns
259: # - Look for same EventSource/WebSocket lifecycle in multiple files
260: # - Look for same timer/reconnection logic in multiple stores
261: 
262: # 2. Create service in lib/services/
263: # - File: web/src/lib/services/{name}.ts
264: # - Export factory function: createXxxConnection(options)
265: # - Options include callbacks for domain-specific handling
266: 
267: # 3. Update consumers to use service
268: # - Import factory function
269: # - Replace inline infrastructure with service calls
270: # - Keep domain-specific event handlers in store
271: 
272: # 4. Verify
273: # - Build: cd web && bun run build
274: # - Check duplicate patterns removed: grep for old patterns
275: ```
276: 
277: ---
278: 
279: ## Debugging Checklist
280: 
281: Before spawning an investigation about extraction issues:
282: 
283: 1. **Check kb:** `kb context "extract"`
284: 2. **Check this guide:** You're reading it
285: 3. **Check git log:** `git log --oneline -10` for recent extractions
286: 4. **Check build:** `go build ./cmd/orch/` for immediate errors
287: 5. **Check tests:** `go test ./cmd/orch/...` for regressions
288: 
289: If those don't answer your question, then investigate. But update this guide with what you learn.
290: 
291: ---
292: 
293: ## Line Count Benchmarks
294: 
295: From successful extractions:
296: 
297: | Extraction | Before | After | Reduction |
298: |------------|--------|-------|-----------|
299: | serve_agents.go from serve.go | 2921 | 1815 | -1106 |
300: | status_cmd.go from main.go | 4964 | 3906 | -1058 |
301: | clean_cmd.go from main.go | ~1000 | ~330 | -670 |

113: The model's claimed fix (spawn dedup via SpawnedIssueTracker) only prevents the daemon from spawning the SAME issue ID twice. It doesn't prevent:
